data.title: GREEN: Implement LINK event handler in process_outbound()
data.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Description
Implement the LINK event handler as an elif branch in process_outbound() in bridge-outbound.py. This handler processes LINK events to sync local relates_to relationships to Jira as "Relates" links.

## Implementation Details

1. Add elif event_type == "LINK": branch in process_outbound()
2. Resolve source Jira key: read SYNC file from source ticket dir (existing pattern from STATUS handler)
3. Resolve target Jira key: read target_id from event data, then read SYNC file from tickets_root / target_id dir. If no SYNC file exists, write bridge_alert and skip
4. Validate link type: call acli_client.get_issue_link_types() on first LINK event, cache result for sync run. Find "Relates" by name. If not found, write bridge_alert with enumerated available types and skip
5. Pre-create dedup: call acli_client.get_issue_links(source_jira_key), check if a "Relates" link already exists between source and target. If exists, skip creation
6. Create link: call acli_client.set_relationship(source_jira_key, target_jira_key, "Relates")
7. Graceful degradation: wrap set_relationship in try/except for CalledProcessError, write bridge_alert on failure, continue processing other events
8. Write SYNC event for traceability (following existing SYNC event pattern)

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/scripts/bridge-outbound.py (Edit: add LINK elif branch in process_outbound)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] process_outbound handles LINK events
  Verify: grep -q 'event_type == "LINK"' plugins/dso/scripts/bridge-outbound.py
- [ ] All RED tests from task d482-067c now pass
  Verify: poetry run pytest tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py -k "link" --tb=short -q
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
  Verify: poetry run pytest tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py --tb=short -q
